corpus_recall_loss_top_reasons.next_capability: receiver-domain evidence instead of selector spelling
source-surface contracts for constructs, macros, and operators
classification cleanup; full corpus exposes an attribution gap absent from crates

hard_gate.evidence: reporting/source-prevalence-only; no semantic code path is changed by this census

obligation_taxonomy.description: Callback/block is invoked in the surrounding synchronous evaluation but still needs identity, cardinality, and effect obligations.
Callback is delayed until iterator/view consumption, so callback errors/effects cannot be treated as eager.
Callback is invoked eagerly by a collection/HOF surface and may expose callback errors/effects immediately.
Callback result is ignored or the surrounding API is effect-oriented; exact value admission must not consume the callback result.
Callback participates in accumulator/result state and needs accumulator demand/effect semantics.
Callback/executor runs as part of constructing a protocol object such as a Promise.
Surface carries success/empty/error/result channels that cannot be collapsed into a plain value.
Surface can throw, rescue, or non-locally transfer control.
Surface carries Promise/Future-style rejection or error continuation behavior.
Surface crosses a task/thread/microtask/async protocol boundary.
Surface may cancel, stop early, or short-circuit control; currently represented by adjacent callback/channel buckets when observed.
Surface creates, views, materializes, consumes, or aliases iterator/stream/collection lifecycle state.
Surface mutates a receiver/place or exposes mutation-sensitive effects.
Selector/property spelling is visible but receiver/symbol/domain proof is missing.
Classified but outside the initial #594 obligation families.

recommendations.why: The largest observed boundary is JS/TS Promise async/scheduling at 29,094 occurrences, but it is explicitly unsafe to admit without shared obligations.
recommendations.work: Design minimal scheduling/channel/callback obligation vocabulary before producer work

recommendations.why: The full corpus priority census has mutation/effect, unsupported-runtime, and HOF demand/effect buckets that should become measurable before any widening.
recommendations.work: Move broad recall-loss buckets into structured scheduling/channel/callback sub-buckets

recommendations.why: Receiver mutation, callback timing, scheduling, and error/rejection channels are exactly the false-merge risks exposed by the census.
recommendations.work: Build cross-language hard negatives for the top observed obligation families

recommendations.why: Callback-shaped obligations total 18,637 occurrences across several languages and reuse existing HOF demand/effect concepts.
recommendations.work: Start producer work with callback demand/effect reporting, not broad async admission

recommendations.why: High frequency is pricing evidence only; scheduling, exception, rejection, aggregate result, and callback-effect obligations are missing.
recommendations.work: Keep Promise/Future/async/channel convergence closed until channel and scheduling evidence exists

source_policy: source prevalence is pricing evidence only; it never proves semantic admission

top_surfaces.note: Option Some constructor has pack-proven occurrence support.
await needs async scheduling, exception, and effect contracts before convergence
Result Ok constructor has pack-proven occurrence support.
Option None sentinel has pack-proven occurrence support.
async functions produce Promise-like protocol surfaces with scheduling effects
vec! macro factories have source-syntax and pack-proven occurrence support.
Result Err constructor has pack-proven occurrence support.
count is admitted only with collection/array receiver proof
iterator HOF needs receiver, callback, and demand/effect proof
range builtin
materializes admitted lazy iterator producers
iterator identity adapter result-domain proof
builder append and receiver mutation evidence exist under effect-gated proof
effect-oriented block iteration
collection factory; single-argument calls stay closed unless the argument has array-domain proof
length is admitted only for exact array/string/property-builtin receiver proof
append mutates collection state
collect remains type-directed; it does not assert collection result domain alone
map get needs exact map receiver proof and downstream result use
materializes admitted lazy iterator producers distinctly
collection membership needs exact receiver proof
raise exception
synchronous Enumerable callback
rescue exception channel
receiver mutation evidence exists; exact value admission stays closed after mutation
